name: Chocolate Ganache
recipeInstructions:
1. Split the vanilla bean lengthwise and add it to the cream, along with the salt and ground coffee.
2. Bring to a gentle boil over medium heat, then continue boiling for one minute.
3. Remove from heat and let cool for a few minutes.
4. Strain through a fine sieve into a mixing bowl.
5. Melt the chocolate carefully using a double boiler (exercise caution).
6. Add the melted chocolate to the hot cream and whisk until well blended.
7. Add the liqueurs and whisk, then add the butter and whisk again.
Note: If this ganache is for truffles, it needs to be frozen overnight. For other uses like cake filling, refrigerate it for a good amount of time to allow it to firm up. You can omit the vanilla beans and substitute Cognac for Kahlua if desired.
